mnemonics: G.R.I.D. - Guided, Regular, Iterative, Discretization. A methodical grid search for optimal parameters.
keyPoints: Discretizes the hyperparameter space into a grid of possible values.
Performs an exhaustive search over every combination of grid points to find the best model.
Simple and parallelizable but computationally expensive, especially for a high number of parameters.
commonMistakes: Making the grid too fine-grained, which leads to an explosion in the number of combinations and intractable computation time.
Using grid search for parameter types that are not easily discretized (e.g., continuous parameters with a very wide range).
Forgetting that grid search only evaluates the points on the grid and can miss the optimal value if it lies between grid points.
